title: Finish Maximum Jobs
hints: This is a classic activity selection problem that can be solved using a greedy approach.
Sort the jobs by their finish time (B[i]) in ascending order.
Always select the job that finishes earliest among the available jobs.
After selecting a job, only consider jobs that start after the current job finishes.
Keep track of the end time of the last selected job.
For each job, if its start time is >= the end time of the last selected job, select it.
The greedy choice is optimal: selecting the job that finishes earliest leaves maximum time for remaining jobs.
